mapping.a_element: extended particles of size l>=1 hopping on a 1D lattice
mapping.b_element: ribosomes (each covering several codons) moving along the mRNA track

mapping.a_element: lattice sites (units of the lattice spacing)
mapping.b_element: codon positions along the mRNA track

mapping.a_element: totally asymmetric forward hopping at a given rate
mapping.b_element: unidirectional ribosome translocation set by the mechanochemical cycle rate

mapping.a_element: hard-core/steric exclusion of the extended objects on the lattice
mapping.b_element: steric exclusion of different ribosomes on the same mRNA track
average ribosome velocity / flux of protein synthesis along the track

mapping.a_element: density profiles and phase behavior of the driven system
mapping.b_element: phase diagram of ribosome traffic in the space of controllable parameters

brief_justification: Both are driven totally-asymmetric simple exclusion processes (TASEP) of extended hard-core objects hopping unidirectionally on a 1D lattice: paper A studies the l>=1 TASEP's stationary current, density profiles, and phase behavior, and paper B explicitly incorporates 'steric exclusion of different individuals on the same track' and draws a 'phase diagram of ribosome traffic', so the collective steady-state exclusion-process machinery corresponds role-for-role (particles<->ribosomes, hopping<->translocation, current<->synthesis flux).
